paragraphs.body: The Playground of Unspeakable Horror

annotations.comment: The story seems so long but then I realized you used different sized fonts with a lot of space in between lines. The title — hmm. It does feel a little Simpsons-y? But maybe you want that feeling.

paragraphs.body: 1. locked in the locker
"Oh, it's okay Suzie," Henry said behind the slits of the locker. "It's just part of a game we play."
"Are you sure you don't want me to get the custodian?" She asked, "He can break the lock, you know."
"Oh, don't worry about it. It wouldn't be much of a game if they didn't come back to let me out. They're my friends, you know. We play all the time."

"Well, okay," Suzie said, looking side to side, seeing that the halls were pretty much empty, "If you say they are coming back for you. Well," she said shyly, "I've got to get going to cheerleading practice. But it was nice talking to you."

annotations.comment: I like the premise here — and enjoyed the conversation (I am less optimistic that Suzie is about his friends’ return). But I do want the narrator to help me understand the situation a bit more. “Henry, a sophomore at Richland High School, said…” Just a few more hints to get us the information to follow along.
annotations.context_left: "Well, okay," Suzie said, looking side to side, seeing that the halls were pretty much empty, "If
annotations.context_right: say they are coming back for you. Well," she said shyly, "I've got to get going to cheerleading pra

paragraphs.body: "Yeah, I'll see you around, Suzie." And with that, he heard her walk away, her sneakers delicately pitter-pattering on the- "Oh, Suzie, one more thing!" He heard the pitter-pattering stop. "Suzie! I forgot to tell you something." There was another moment of silence. But then the pitter pattering of her sneaker returned, quicker this time. Only it kept getting quieter, and quieter...

annotations.comment: Oh, I was surprised she abandoned him.
annotations.context_left: nce. But then the pitter pattering of her sneaker returned, quicker this time. Only it kept getting

paragraphs.body: He wanted to tell her that he thought her new haircut looked nice, even through the slits of the locker. He could see out those slits if he angled his head the right way, although he couldn't hold the position long without cramping his neck. Now there wasn't much to see. Now the hallway was completely empty. And now- Du du du- the lights turned off.
2. date night

annotations.comment: I like this formatting choice. The titles help us follow the story and the time jumps.

paragraphs.body: A few hours later, Suzie sat shotgun in Dave's car, a white Mustang his parents bought him for taking their team to states. She wore his varsity jacket now, still in her practice leotard, shivering timidly as the car warmed up. "You didn’t stuff that kid in the locker, did you?" she asked.

annotations.comment: Nice. Gets us right to the point.

paragraphs.body: "What kid are you talking about?" Dave asked, toggling the radio. Country wasn't quite the mood he was looking for, nor was Gospel- but apparently they were the only two stations that reached their town.
"Oh you know, that scrawny kid with the buggy eyes. I think his name’s Henry. You know who I'm talking about. He sits front row at all of your games, or maybe he's in the band, kind of a quiet."
"Hmm, couldn’t say. You know I stuff a lot of kids in lockers these days," he turned to her with a dry smile. "Now that you say it, I think I'm running out of lockers. Maybe I can borrow yours?"
"Oh, ha ha," she said, "Yes, you can borrow mine. Only if you can get the heat to work in this stupid car. I feel like my fingers are falling off." She suddenly grabbed his hands to demonstrate.
"Alright alright," he said, shaking them off.
“But you didn’t do it right?” asked Suzie.
“Come on Suzie, do you really think I’m that kind of guy?” he replied.

annotations.comment: Oh, interesting. I like that she doubles down on this. I thought perhaps she was aware that he had done it.

paragraphs.body: “It’s just, I’ve seen you push him around before, that’s all.”

Dave sighed, “Suzie, as a man, there are some things you can’t just let fly, alright? And sometimes, people need to be pushed around as a matter of… education, okay? But that doesn’t make me a monster just because I have principles.”
She grabbed his hands again and brought her face real close to his, all doggy-eyed, “But I didn’t say you’re a monster, I love your principles, my big, strong, man…”

annotations.comment: This felt like too much to me.

paragraphs.body: Her cold hands caused a chill to run down his spine, and he brushed her off once more. It was just a reflex, but to her, it felt like something more.
“You hate me, don’t you Dave?” she asked.

annotations.comment: Wow! This conversation has almost a David Lynch feel to it — nearly surreal.

paragraphs.body: “Let’s get something to eat.”
3. hunger

It was Jeremy Strickhand's (linebacker) locker, and it didn't smell too bad, only faintly of the pot which got him temporarily (linebacker) suspended, of which a feeble eighth escaped confiscation by hiding in his jacket pocket. As far as lockers went, Jeremy Strickhand’s was one of the finer ones he’d been stuffed into. Usually lockers were filled with nonsense- textbooks, clothes, rotting-forgotten lunches- but Jeremy Stickhand’s had little such nonsense, as though he didn’t care much for the curriculum. But that meant nothing jutting uncomfortably into Henry’s kidneys, nothing forcing him to contort his body in foreign ways, only a humble jacket to soften the hard walls of the locker. He considered it a business-class experience.

annotations.comment: You might get at this in the prior scene. It felt like Dave shoves a lot of people into lockers, but this makes it sound like it’s more like Henry gets shoved into lockers a lot.
annotations.context_left: ket pocket. As far as lockers went, Jeremy Strickhand’s was one of the finer ones he’d been stuffed
annotations.context_right: . Usually lockers were filled with nonsense- textbooks, clothes, rotting-forgotten lunches- but Jere

paragraphs.body: “But I’m starting to get hungry,” he said to himself. “And if Dave doesn’t come back soon, then I’ll be stuck here for the weekend.” Henry shook his head defiantly, “No, but he wouldn’t do that. It’s like he always says, I just need to lighten up.”

annotations.comment: He doesn’t have a cell phone? Or someone took it? Or they don’t exist yet?

paragraphs.body: But the school’s heating had been shut off hours ago, and the locker didn’t provide any relevant insulation, “Maybe when Dave gets back he’ll lend me his varsity jacket,” then he remembered Jeremy’s jacket in the locker, “Well, I don’t suppose he’d mind,” and struggled to manipulate his arms into the sleeves, despite his limited mobility.

Once he had properly covered himself, he let out a sigh of relief, then reclined himself against the back wall of the locker. He looked out the slits of the locker and into the now completely dark hallway. He thought about how Suzie had offered to get a custodian to free him from this prison. It’s just a game, he had said. How many hours ago had that been?
This is fun, isn’t it, he thought as his eyes began to well with tears. Oh, how much fun this is. Dave and I always have so much fun together, don’t we?

Over the sounds of his own sobs, Henry could not hear the gentle pitter-pattering resounding through the hallway. The sound of an approaching group of footsteps, sneakers falling like gentle raindrops.
“What’s the locker number?” asked a muted voice.

annotations.comment: Intriguing. It sort of seemed backwards that once he gets the jacket on and warms up, that’s when he starts crying? Maybe it’s the reverse — he starts crying then comforts himself by putting on the jacket after thinking he shouldn’t?

paragraphs.body: 4.   infidelity
“I’ve got to make a phone call, Suzie.”

“You don’t need my permission,” she said, before taking another chomp from her Whopper. They were the only ones in the Burger King, besides the cashier who didn’t want to be there. There is an eerie quality about an empty playpen illuminated by the moon. There were better places to be on a Friday night.
“I mean I need to step outside. It’s a personal sort of call.”
“So, you're leaving me?”
“Just for a minute, Suzie, I’ve got to make a phone call.”
“I get it. You’ve got to call your other girlfriend.”
“What? I just gotta make a phone call, that’s all.”
“Then why don’t you make it here?”

annotations.comment: Great dialogue, but I did feel like these two are slightly more angry at each other than the last time I saw them. Their dynamic feels different, too. Previously, she seemed a little scared of him, and here she seems to feel more comfortable being angry at him.

paragraphs.body: “It’ll only be a second, jeez, can’t I get a second? We’ve got the whole night ahead of us.”

“Don’t you know what kind of people come to Burger King, asshole, you could’ve at least taken me to the Olive Garden if you were going to leave me here all night. Drive off with your other girl, probably take her to a movie-“

“I get it. Just give me a second,” and he stood up from his colorful seat, and walked out the door. It was slightly too cold for comfort, and through the window Suzie extended him a middle finger. He gave Tucker a ring.
“Yo, Dave, when are you stopping by?”
“Listen bro, I need you to do me a favor,” said Dave.
“I don’t know if now’s the time for a favor,” chuckles in the background, hardly audible, “Jeremy Strickhand just stopped by.”
“Remember that creep who’s always following us around?” asked Dave.
“Yeah, what, did he tell the school or something?”
“No, no-” said Dave.
“Then what’s the problem?”
“I never let him out of the locker.”
“Oh shit, haha.”
“I meant to, but then he said some shit that rubbed me the wrong way, you know…”
“What’d he say?” asked Tucker.
“He said he thought my hair looked nice. I was like, what’s that supposed to mean?” Dave exclaimed.

annotations.comment: OF COURSE homophobia is involved. Again, this dialogue is great.

paragraphs.body: “So you left him in the locker for the weekend?” asked Tucker.

“And I need you guys to get him out. Because I’m at Burger King with Suzie right now, and if I bail on her tonight she’ll kill me. And if the school finds about this locker situation, I’m going to get kicked off the fucking team!”
“You’ll get more than kicked off the team if that kid starves to death in there- Hey, if you’re at Burger King, are you trynna pick us up something?”
“Come on dude… I need you guys to get to the school right now, this is not time to bullshit around,” Dave commanded.

annotations.comment: I think it might help the story if he really fulsomely denies stuffing Henry in the locker to Suzie. Maybe that’s why he can’t just take her back with him. (I also wondered if she forgets about Henry or just doesn’t care or what)

paragraphs.body: “Dude,” said Tucker on the other side of the telephone, steeping into the couch like the bong water stains in the carpet. “That is not even remotely possible right now.”

When Dave re-entered the Burger King, he saw Suzie was no longer in her seat. Where’d she go, he thought. He saw the glow of the moon as it shone on the playpen, then he heard a tumbling from within the slide. When she reached the bottom, she jumped up laughing, then looked at Dave. She stopped laughing.
“When did we stop having fun?” she asked.

annotations.comment: Ouch.

paragraphs.body: 5. back in the halls of westmore high
“Shit dude, shit… I think I hear something in there…”
“You’re tripping bro, what do you even mean-”
“Just shut the fuck up a second, alright, listen.”

annotations.comment: Who’s speaking here? I’m not sure who actually ended up going.

paragraphs.body: “Alright, chill out-”
From within the locker, the voices outside the locker heard a strange, ghostly cry- I want to go home, I just want to go home.

annotations.comment: Here, I was surprised, because the last time you showed us Henry, he was calming.
annotations.context_left: From within the locker, the voices outside the locker heard a strange, ghostly cry- I want to
annotations.context_right: home, I just want to go home.

paragraphs.body: “Dude, what the fuck.”
“What should we do?”
“I don’t know, fucking knock?”
“Alright, alright.”
BANG, BANG, BANG.
From inside the locker, “Dave, is it you? Oh, I was getting so scared, you finally came back for me!”
“No, its not fucking Dave, who are you?” asked the outside voice.
“Oh. I’m Henry, Dave is my best friend.”

annotations.comment: This also felt like a more extreme character. Had trouble buying it.

paragraphs.body: “Then what are you doing in there?”
“Dave put me in here.”
Through the slits of the locker, Henry could make out three figures in ski masks. He watched as the took a couple steps back, and seemed to huddle together.
What the fuck dude, said one of them, out of range for Henry to hear. He put someone in there to guard the fucking weed?
Dave is Jeremy’s friend. Jeremy really is as fucked up in the head as they say he is, shit.
Do you think he has a gun or something?
Why the fuck would he have a gun?

annotations.comment: Switching back and forth between italics and quotation marks is a little confusing but at the same time I like how it shows that perspective switch.

paragraphs.body: Why the fuck would he be in the locker, dumbass- he probably has a gun or something.
Then there’s gotta be a lot of weed in there, right?
Dude, I knew this was a bad idea, we gotta get the fuck out of here.
No dude. Be cool. Just follow my lead.
“Don’t do anything smart, man, we’ve all got guns!”
“Oh shit,” cried Henry, “Why do you all have guns?”

annotations.comment: Honestly, I LOL’d.

paragraphs.body: “Don’t do anything dumb,” said one of the voices, “When we open the locker, just don’t try anything funny.”
“I don’t intend to!” replied Henry, unsure what was left to do if he couldn’t do anything smart, and couldn’t do anything dumb. It seemed to leave him with no option.
Alright, whispered one of the voices, when we break the lock, just start swinging at him before he has a chance to do anything. We’ll grab the weed and run.
“Hello?” cried out Henry.
BANG, BANG, BANG!!!!!
Then a moment’s silence.
Then the locker creaked as it fell open, followed by an assault of fists and feet and knees…

annotations.comment: It sounds like Henry hears them plan to hurt him so I wondered why he didn’t respond to that. In general, I think you need to help us understand who these four are a bit more. I also wondered if there is actually a lot of weed in the locker and if not, why they think there would be (enough to assault someone over)!

paragraphs.body: 6. the beginning of the end
“So you’re just going to leave me off?” asked Suzie, stunned.
“It’s not that I want to. There is just something I have to take care of,” replied Dave at the wheel of his white mustang as he pulled into Suzie’s driveway.
“But what about the movie, you said we would watch a movie?”
“I know I said that. But this is imp-”
“Oh, I bet its important, this other girl of yours- I’ll bet she’s a whole lot more important than me.”

annotations.comment: Again, maybe this could come up earlier. I’m still wondering what she thinks happened to Henry.

paragraphs.body: “No, come on. It’s nothing like that.”
“You’re going to need to prove it's nothing like that. Holy shit Dave, you are such an asshole, I knew I deserved better than you.”
“Suzie, come on, don’t be like that.”
She unbuckled her seatbelt, and reached for the door-
“Wait until I stop the fucking car, relax for a second.”
7. the end

Dave drove furiously in his white mustang to the rear parking lot of Westmore High. He got out of his car and entered the school through the locker room, which was always left open in case any football players wanted to use the gym, or any of the school's facilities on the weekend.

annotations.comment: Nice detail.

paragraphs.body: He left the locker room and proceeded down the halls, heading straight for Jeremy Strickhand’s locker, 4390. It was as he turned into this hallway that he first heard the sounds of an altercation.
“I can’t fucking find it! This locker’s empty.”
Thud, thud, thud.
“It’s gotta be there, come on man, our sources don’t lie.”
Thud, thud, thud.

“What the hell’s going on there?” Shouted Dave across the hall. He could make out three figures in the hallway, standing over something. Freshman, no doubt. I’ll bet Jeremy owes them some money or something. He’d tell those morons a tylenol was a percocet.
“Oh shit!” shouted one.
“It’s Dave!” shouted another.
“Get him!” shouted a third, and they stopped kicking the limp body on the floor, and began to charge in Dave’s direction.

Now, Dave began to charge in their direction too, and at six foot three, 240 pounds, they stood little chance. The first one to reach him stuttered a step before he did, and Dave laid a shoulder into his jaw, sending him flying into the brick wall which separated groups of lockers- his head colliding in a crunchy CRRACCCK.

The next one threw a right hook into Dave’s ribs, but the assailant withdrew his hand and stared at it, unsure whether his ring or middle finger was broken. Then Dave clocked him with a straight to the temple, and down he went.

The third saw what happened to his friends and quickly began to run in the other way, but Dave sprinted after him, then jumped. He grabbed hold of the ski mask, and brought him down with the force of gravity- followed by several punches to the side of the head.

annotations.comment: Great scene!

paragraphs.body: Dave stood up. He examined the carnage around him. The first freshman was beginning to stand up, uneasily, stumbling with each step.
“Now scram!” shouted Dave, and the three stumbled to their feet and ran.

On the floor by Jeremy Strickhand’s locker, Dave saw Henry lying defeated. His eyes were both blackened, his right ear nearly peeled off. Dave approached him and knelt on the ground beside him. Then he began to cradle his head, and look deeply into his blackened eyes. Tears began to well.
“I never meant to hurt you,” said Dave, “It’s just that Suzie was nagging me. I never meant to leave you in there so long…”

annotations.comment: I buy this but let us see it a bit more.

paragraphs.body: “It’s okay Dave,” choked Henry, “I forgive you.”
“But look at what they did to you… What I did to you.”
“It’s okay Dave. I’m just happy to see you again.”
“I was just so scared. That’s my problem. I’m always so scared, I didn’t want the guys to suspect anything…”
“I forgive you Dave. I understand.”
Then Dave perched his head over Henry’s and kissed him on the lips.

annotations.comment: I liked this ending but had trouble buying the kiss. It was just a bit too far if Henry is truly a bloody mess. That said, wow, what a strong story, Everly! I really enjoyed it! Very nice work. Besides some small formatting choices, I think the biggest thing to work on is making sure the opening scene set up the dynamics you want the characters to have by the end. I’ve asked the questions above, so I won’t repeat too much, but the dynamic between Suzie and Dave feels particularly strong. I wondered, for example, if Suzie wouldn’t be about to help Henry and then get ordered away by Dave. Or maybe she’d tell Henry she’s going to go get Dave and make him fix things and then somehow get distracted… etc. Happy to talk through this with you if you like. Strong work!
